job.title: Tools Software Engineer
job.description: Mandatory: Experience in Python, R, JavaScript, or Perl programming; or UNIX scripting (U)Experience integrating with the OpenText Enterprise Management tools suite (U)Must be a self-starter with strong attention to detail (U)Proficient in Linux/UNIX commands (U)Proficient in Windows Server OS (U)Desired: Application/SA Experience with the OpenText Ops Bridge Suite (U)Experience with OpenText Application Performance Management (APM) (U)Experience with OpenText Business Process Monitor (BPM) (U)Experience with OpenText OPTIC Data Lake (U)Experience with OpenText Operations Bridge Reporter (OBR) (U)Experience with operating data analytics apps (Splunk or Elastic ELK) (U)Strong organizational, communication, and collaboration skills (U)Special Requirements (U)DoD 8570 IA Type: IAT Level 2 (U)DoD 8570 CE CompTIA Security+ CE (U)Certification (U)Requirements: (U)DoD 8570 CE Training *Training Not Acceptable to Meet CE Requirement* (U)Requirements: https://epmo1.gdit.com/nis_epmo.nsf/HCPrint/06BE449E935F539785258B3B0062CF8C?OpenDocument 2/3 (U)1/8/25, 9:35 AM Billet - GWGES-0082 (U)Key Personnel Pager Required Holiday Work CONUS Travel (U)Privileged User Mission Critical Shift Work OCONUS Travel (U)POV Weekend Work Warzone Travel (U)COOP Night Work Unpublished (U)Requirements (U)Special Requirement (U)Notes: (U)OCI Requirements (U)Employee Compliance Statement Required (U)NDA Required (U)Firewall Employee (U)Firewall NDA Required (U)Debriefing Required (U)Annual OCI Training Required (U)Annual OCI Firewall Training Required (U)Third Party NDA Required (U)Customer NDA Required (U)Customer Signed NDA Required (U)Other: (U)Comments (U)Attachments (U)Attachment Type File Name Status Uploaded Uploaded By (U)No Entries Found (U)Nominations (U)Status Person Date (U)No Entries Found https://epmo1.gdit.com/nis_epmo.nsf/HCPrint/06BE449E935F539785258B3B0062CF8C?OpenDocument 3/3

job.title: OneDrive Storage Engineer (M365)
job.description: Mandatory: Ability to work independently and collaboratively in a team-oriented environment (U)Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with a customer-focused approach to service delivery (U)Experience with data replication, backup, and disaster recovery strategies in both cloud and on- premises environments (U)Expertise in migrating data to Microsoft 365 (M365) and OneDrive, with a solid understanding of cloud migration methodologies (U)In-depth knowledge of NetApp storage systems, including configuration, administration, and performance tuning (U)Proven experience as a Senior Storage Engineer or similar role, specializing in NetApp storage solutions (U)Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with the ability to troubleshoot complex storage issues (U)Desired: (U)Special Requirements (U)DoD 8570 IA Type: IAT Level 2 https://epmo1.gdit.com/nis_epmo.nsf/HCPrint/979A3AD98155AF8585258B770062F642?OpenDocument 2/3 (U)1/8/25, 9:38 AM Billet - GWGES-0117 (U)DoD 8570 CE CompTIA Security+ CE (U)Certification (U)Requirements: (U)DoD 8570 CE Training (U)Requirements: (U)Key Personnel Pager Required Holiday Work CONUS Travel (U)Privileged User Mission Critical Shift Work OCONUS Travel (U)POV Weekend Work Warzone Travel (U)COOP Night Work Unpublished (U)Requirements (U)Special Requirement (U)Notes: (U)OCI Requirements (U)Employee Compliance Statement Required (U)NDA Required (U)Firewall Employee (U)Firewall NDA Required (U)Debriefing Required (U)Annual OCI Training Required (U)Annual OCI Firewall Training Required (U)Third Party NDA Required (U)Customer NDA Required (U)Customer Signed NDA Required (U)Other: (U)Comments (U)Attachments (U)Attachment Type File Name Status Uploaded Uploaded By (U)No Entries Found (U)Nominations (U)Status Person Date (U)No Entries Found https://epmo1.gdit.com/nis_epmo.nsf/HCPrint/979A3AD98155AF8585258B770062F642?OpenDocument 3/3

job.title: Product Convergence Architect
job.description: Experience supporting enterprise rollouts and managing stakeholder expectations. (U)Previous experience consolidating teams or systems across enterprise programs. https://freedomconsultinggroup.sharepoint.com/sites/DragonConfettiStaffing2/Lists/DC Vacancies/DispForm.aspx?ID=279&Source=https%3A%2F%2F… 3/5 (U)2/19/26, 9:12 AM Openings - 0354-06 (U)Experience orchestrating vendor engagements and evaluating vendor technical roadmaps. (U)Understanding of document/content management solutions (U)(Feith, etc.) even if not the focus of the role. (U)Qualifications (U)TS/SCI with Polygraph (U)Twelve (12) years of experience in software development/engineering (U)Bachelor’s degree from an accredited college or university in Computer Science or related discipline. Four (4) years of additional software development experience may be substituted for a bachelor’s degree. (U)Master’s degree from an accredited college or university in Computer Science or related discipline can substitute for (U)Two (2) years of experience. (U)Ability to analyze customer requirements, identify system improvements, and effectively troubleshoot issues to enhance application performance and user experience. (U)Experience with AWS (U)Excellent communication and interpersonal skills to engage with customers, understand their needs, and provide timely and effective support, ensuring high levels of satisfaction. (U)General understanding of Microsoft environments and enterprise collaboration tools (U)Experience with FEITH Records (U)Management System or other COTS https://freedomconsultinggroup.sharepoint.com/sites/DragonConfettiStaffing2/Lists/DC Vacancies/DispForm.aspx?ID=279&Source=https%3A%2F%2F… 4/5 (U)2/19/26, 9:12 AM Openings - 0354-06 products. (U)Development or scripting experience to support system configuration or automation tasks. (U)Technical awareness and problem- solving ability related to system integration and configuration. (U)Strong communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to onboard and train users on a new system. (U)Experience supporting or working with commercial off-the-shelf (COTS) products or integrations. (U)Ability to work independently while collaborating with developers and product support teams. (U)Must be able to work during core hours of (U)Monday-Friday 10am-2pm. Exceptions will be considered on a case-by-case basis. # (U)Share or copy a link that gives people access to (U)The focus of this TTO is cloud conversion of CMS capabilities. This this page, or send a summary of the page with a task includes the planlninink gfo, rs soitfetw maerme bdeervs.elopment, data management, software recoding, analytic rewriting, testing, and deployment activities necessary to assess, modify, migrate, integrate, test, and deploy current and new capabilities into CMS’ cloud environments. (U)Converting capabilities to operate in cloud environments includes, but is not limited to, the following activities: (U)Assess capabilities to identify potential approaches to convert the capabilities for operating in a cloud environment (U)Assess capabilities to identify data migration, software modification, legal, security, and compliance requirements (U)SRE Hosting associated with converting the capability to a cloud environment (U)2 (U)Support (U)Develop a detailed data migration plan outlining procedures for migrating data from the capability into the cloud environment (U)Assess capabilities to identify algorithms and analytics changes that could improve efficiency and/or outcomes by converting the algorithms/analytics to cloud operations (U)Design, develop, and test the software changes needed to convert the capability using the approach selected by the Government (U)Design, develop, test, and execute the data modifications/migrations needed to convert the capability using the approach selected by the Government (U)Develop, test, and coordinate a deployment plan to implement the changes (U)Tech Stack: Cloud Experience, Cloud data and Software Migration, (U)Software Development (U)5 DevOps The focus of this TTO is to stabilize and improve Corporate (U)Management Services (CMS) systems to further CMS’ mission of providing business and administrative capabilities to the NSA/CSS enterprise. A significant component of the stabilizing and https://freedomconsultinggroup.sharepoint.com/sites/DragonConfettiStaffing2/SitePages/DRAGONCONFETTI-TTO-Overviews.aspx 1/17 (U)4/2/25, 1:11 PM DRAGONCONFETTI TTO Overviews improving activities involves moving existing systems into CMS’ (U)DevOps software life-cycle management processes pushing builds through CMS’s Continuous Integration/Continuous Deployment (U)(CI/CD) pipeline and converting existing systems to use CMS’ (U)DevStack of technologies. (U)Stabilizing and improving operational systems includes, but is not limited to, the following activities: (U)Assess systems to identify the type and maturity of the software development, testing, documentation, and deployment processes and artifacts (U)Identify gaps and discrepancies between the current processes and system artifacts and CMS’ DevOps processes, procedures, and artifacts (U)Design, develop, and test the software changes needed to incorporate DevOps practices into systems (U)Design, develop, test, and execute the data modifications/migrations needed to incorporate the changes (U)Implement and deploy the changes (U)Understand and utilize Application Programming Interfaces (APIs) and abstraction layers to be able to connect to different systems of record in accordance with changes in the foundational CMS source systems (U)Assess and monitor the deployed changes and correct any technical issues (U)Identify, collect, and analyze metrics relevant to the success, impact, and progress of the system stabilization (U)CMS Stabilization (U)Tech Stack: Automated Testing, Software (U)Development/improvement, Data Migration, API (U)Development (U)CMS Transition (U)Tech Stack: Docker, Mongo DB, Node.js, CentOS, Packer, (U)Terraform, Jenkins, Consul, Vault, Virtual Machines, AWS (U)6 RMSV The focus of this TTO is to perform support and development for (U)Electronic Records Management Services (RMS) including the current STONEVAULT application and its related and integrated application SETINSTONE. (U)The STONEVAULT application provides a modern, secure, leading- edge capability compliant with external regulatory guidance including electronic maintenance, storage, search, audit, reporting, retrieval, lockdown, and disposition of records, considered both permanent and temporary. (U)Tech Stack: Project Management, System Engineering, (U)Software Engineering, Config Management, Security, (U)Windows, Linux, Java, Records Management Technology, https://freedomconsultinggroup.sharepoint.com/sites/DragonConfettiStaffing2/SitePages/DRAGONCONFETTI-TTO-Overviews.aspx 2/17 (U)4/2/25, 1:11 PM DRAGONCONFETTI TTO Overviews (U)Apache, Amazon CodeDeploy/AWS KMS, Oracle HP 1AP, (U)SharePoint, SQL and SQL Server (U)The focus of the SETINSTONE effort is to provide the expanded capability by building upon and integrating with the current (U)STONEVAULT RMS system. (U)Tech Stack: Project Management, System Engineering, (U)Software Engineering, Config Management, Security, (U)Windows, Linux, Java, Records Management Technologyu, (U)Apache, Amazon CodeDeploy/AWS KMS, Oracle HP 1AP, (U)Sharepoint, SQL and SQL Server (U)The focus of this TTO is to provide Life Cycle Sustainment (LCS) services to include Maintenance Releases, Help Desk, and System (U)Monitoring activities. Perceptive Content is a document storage solution that provides the ability to capture document images and metadata for record keeping purposes. The organizations supported by systems maintained under this TTO are Installations and Logistics, Human Resource, Business Finance, and Chief of (U)Staff. (U)Perceptive (U)8 Multiple environments will be utilized during this TTO's activities, (U)Content including Development, Test, and Production. (U)Teck Stack: ImageNow, WebNow, SQL, JavaScript, Oracle, (U)WebLogic, PKI/CASPORT (U)OnBase Certification (required): Resources must be certified in the perceptive content/installer software. This certification can be obtained from https:/training.hyland.com, search for Installer (U)Certification (course 0097). The direct website is https://training.hyland.com/courses/10097-1nstaIIer- (U)Certification. (U)10 Human The focus of this TTO is to provide life cycle support for the Human (U)Resources Resource (HR) Applications and Services. (U)Current Applications supported by this TTO include: (U)Candidate Experience (CE): An application that enables the (U)Intelligence Community (IC) and the Agency to receive, search, review, and hire external applicants into the Agency. (U)Tech Stack: JIRA, Confluence, ArcGIS 3D, Analyst Extension, (U)ArcGIS Data Interoperability Extension, ArcGIS Spatial (U)Analyst Extension, ArcGIS Pro, ArcGIS Portal, ArcGIS File (U)Geodatabase, AutoCAD, Autodesk AutoCAD, Autodesk Vault, (U)Geoserver, GitLab, Google Earth Enterprise Platform, Google (U)Earth Desktop Application, Cesium 3D, AJAX, Apache HTTP, (U)CASPORT, CentOS, Cesium3D, Cognos, CSS, Eclipse, (U)Geoserver, HTML/HTML5, IIS, Java/JavaScript, JQuery, JSON, (U)KML, Lyne, MASH, Oracle SQL Developer, Paint.net, (U)PGAdmin, PostgreSQL, Putty, Python, RESTful Web Services, https://freedomconsultinggroup.sharepoint.com/sites/DragonConfettiStaffing2/SitePages/DRAGONCONFETTI-TTO-Overviews.aspx 3/17 (U)4/2/25, 1:11 PM DRAGONCONFETTI TTO Overviews (U)Shell/ksh/csh/bash, Sharepoint, SQL, TLS 1.2, Tomcat, Win (U)10/2012, winSCP (U)Payroll Support Team: The Payroll Support Team shall be responsible for installing and maintaining the DCPS software provided by DFAS as well as in-house written custom queries and reports. DCPS is the standard Department of Defense civilian payroll system. It provides time and attendance, payroll and manpower cost accounting functions. DCPS is used to maintain the official pay and leave records and process the biweekly payroll for civilian personnel. This includes processing and computing various allowances (including foreign allowances), pay differentials (such as Night and Sunday differentials), other pay entitlements (overtime pay, holiday pay, etc.) and other pay associated with certain criteria or job assignments (e.g., Foreign (U)Language Incentive Pay). The DCPS software is designed to process payroll entitlements in accordance with applicable regulatory criteria and/or public law. (U)Tech Stack: Java React (javaScript and typescript) (U)Talent, Evaluation & Advancement (TEA): An application that provides a simple, integrated performance management program at the Agency that provides performance appraisal, promotion, and monetary recognition (bonuses & awards) functionality in a combined system. TEA is a streamlined approach to performance management that prioritizes ongoing, informal performance conversations between managers and employees over extensive paperwork. TEA consists of Assessment Management System (U)(AMS) and Performance Management System (PMS) The application will prioritize ongoing, informal performance conversations between managers and employees to establish a simplified annual timeline (running from January-December) and provide a clear link between individual accomplishments and organizational goals: supporting an agency goal. (U)Tech Stack: JavaScript (U)Access Time: A web application that interfaces with the Time & (U)Labor database in HRMS, that provides Agency civilians with the ability to enter, update, validate and certify their timesheet and submit it to their supervisory management chain for review and approval. (U)Tech Stack: JavaScript (U)WAGGLEENGLISH: A project that provides support to two testing labs: 1) The Human Resources Language Aptitude Testing Lab that provides a mechanism for Agency civilian and military personnel to take both the Defense Language Proficiency Test (DLPT) and the Defense Language Aptitude Battery (DLAB) test, and 2) The (U)Occupational, Health, Environmental, and Safety Services (OHESS) (U)Psychological Assessment Labs that provides a mechanism for prospective Agency civilian personnel to take psychological testing that is a requirement for hiring. https://freedomconsultinggroup.sharepoint.com/sites/DragonConfettiStaffing2/SitePages/DRAGONCONFETTI-TTO-Overviews.aspx 4/17 (U)4/2/25, 1:11 PM DRAGONCONFETTI TTO Overviews (U)Tech Stack: Various COTS products (U)WELLPOINT: A project that provides OHESS's Employee (U)Assistance Services (EAS) organization, case management capability. This functionality is currently provided by a COTS product called Caseware. (U)Tech Stack: .NET (COTS) Visual Basic JavaScript (U)Mishap/lncident Report: An application that provides OHESS's (U)Environmental, Safety and Health Solutions organization with the capability to track, record and report mishaps or incidents involving worker safety or health across the agency. The system provides employees and supervisors direct, self-reporting ability, collecting over 170 separate data points on an event. (U)Tech Stack: Classic ASP (U)Industrial Hygiene: An application that provides OHESS's (U)Environmental, Safety and Health Solutions organization with the capability to track, record and report where workplace hazards, such as chemical and biological exposures, exist and how the (U)Agency is taking steps to mitigate that risk to Agency employees. (U)This system allows for the collection of data on work conditions, risk assessments, recommended controls, inspections, audits and evaluations to support efforts to ensure 100% worker health and safety. This tracking is required by the U.S. Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA) and is reported annually. (U)Laser, Antenna, X-ray and Radioactive Material (LAXR): An inventory system that provides the Agency the ability to track, record and report on the Agency's Laser, Antenna and X-Ray equipment inventory and personal training. (U)Tech Stack: Classic ASP (U)Statistics Package for Social Sciences (SPSS): An application that provides OHESS psychologist with the capability to track and report social science and statistical analysis than enables employee vetting and hiring. (U)Tech Stack: COTS (U)PANORAMA: An application that provides a web-based portal to view datasets from multiple repositories, both corporate and local, to facilitate the resource management and decision-making process for a variety of customers to include managers and watch floor personnel. Travelers is an application within PANORAMA. (U)Travelers provides mission management information via a mapping interface ingesting data from multiple enterprise-wide sources. (U)Tech Stack: Angular, JavaScript, NodeJS (U)11 USSV - The focus of this TTO is to provide life cycle support for the (U)Security Security Sustainment. (U)Services (U)Current Applications supported by this TTO include: https://freedomconsultinggroup.sharepoint.com/sites/DragonConfettiStaffing2/SitePages/DRAGONCONFETTI-TTO-Overviews.aspx 5/17 (U)4/2/25, 1:11 PM DRAGONCONFETTI TTO Overviews (U)Local Police 2 (LP2): An application which provides tracking for alarms, Be On the Look Out (BOLO) notices, equipment, orders, personnel scheduling and weapons qualifications for NSA Police. (U)Tech Stack: Classic ASP (U)Legacy EnablingReach: A COTS (ADS! DataForce) product providing a repository for NSAP Green Sheets, which capture field incidents and vehicle rejections. ER data is transmitted to High (U)Side QTOOL to support Security and Counterintelligence analytics. (U)This system is planned for replacement by WebRMS. (U)Tech Stack: PHP and COBOL (U)Security Awareness: An application used to maintain inventory and track requests for Security and Counterintelligence Awareness products like posters, pamphlets and videos. (U)Tech Stack: Classic ASP (U)Clearance Workflow and Verification System (CWVS): A web-based system encompassing the clearance and adjudication processes for contractors, NSA/CSS applicants, periodic reinvestigations, and military cases. (U)Tech Stack: JAVA (U)Legacy eQIP: This tool is used to submit contractors, NSA/CSS applicants, and military personnel SF86 and answer the questions for reinvestigations. (U)Tech Stack: JAVA (U)Acquisition Security Data Warehouse (ASDW): An application used to gather research on the companies attempting to provide products and services to the Agency. The application stores research and manages the Acquisition workflow. (U)Tech Stack: JavaScript (Mean stack) (U)SCIF Workflow Accreditation and Record Management (U)(SWARM): An application that manages the SCIF accreditation workflow and tracks the SCIF management oversight/inspection process. (U)Tech Stack: JavaScript (Mean stack) (U)Security Incident Report (SIR): An application that tracks Security (U)Incident workflow, specifically the discovery and subsequent confiscation, of prohibited/restricted devices and mishandled classified documents entering or exiting NSA facilities. (U)Tech Stack: JavaScript (Mean stack) (U)Candidate Experience Security Portal: An all-inclusive application used to submit contractor, NSA/CSS applicant, and military personnel SF86 documentation. (U)Tech Stack: JavaScript (Mean stack) (U)WFI/SIP Form: An application that documents clearance and security information of inbound military personnel to the Agency (U)Unclassified Visitor Request (UVR): An application that processes the visitor request form for Unclassified locations. https://freedomconsultinggroup.sharepoint.com/sites/DragonConfettiStaffing2/SitePages/DRAGONCONFETTI-TTO-Overviews.aspx 6/17 (U)4/2/25, 1:11 PM DRAGONCONFETTI TTO Overviews (U)The focus of this TTO is to: (U)Diagnose and resolve/mitigate problems. (U)Provide documentation on architecture, configuration management, root cause analysis, and security related compliance information. (U)Deliver sustainment activities in a manner that increases reliability, (U)15 Infrastructure quality, and efficiency of the government's products and infrastructure services. (U)Tech Stack: Operating Systems: Microsoft Server, Linux Redhat, (U)Linux CentOS, UNIX; Databases: Oracle, Sybase, Microsoft SQL, (U)PostgreSQL databases and Native instance, PostgreSQL databases and Plus Instances, MySQL; Other: System Admin, Disaster (U)Recover, PKI (U)16 Education The focus of this TTO is to provide Life Cycle Sustainment (LCS) and Training activities for commercial and custom-developed education, (U)Sustainment learning, and training applications. Both tasks include end-users and learners that include Agency, non-Agency based military; second party partners (2P), Department of Defense (DOD), and (U)Intelligence Community (IC) affiliates. (U)Current Applications supported by this TTO include: eAssessment: Measures knowledge and skills via exams, quizzes, tests, or surveys and schedules participants to take assessments that a learner has taken at the Agency. (U)Tech Stack: PHP, .net, HTML, CSS, JavaScript, ISS, Jquery, (U)AWS, PowerShell, Windows, Rest, Odata, MSSQL (U)Vuport/New content store: Agency diverse library of mandatory, required, supplemental, and other professional development courseware (U)Tech Stack: .net, HTML, CSS, JavaScript, ISS, Jquery, AWS, (U)Powershell, Windows, Rest, MSSQL eVideo: Web-based, enables users to create, share, video socialize content (U)Tech Stack: Java, JTML, CSS, JavaScript, jboss, Jquery, AWS, (U)LINUX, MSSQL eSurvey: Online survey and assessment tool collects info such as feedback and opinions from users then summarizes them into reports. Agency surveys ongoing throughout the year and ODNI obligations (specific intervals throughout the year). (U)Tech Stack: Java, JTML, CSS, JavaScript, ISS, Jquery, AWS, (U)PowerShell, Windows, Rest, MSSQL eCampus: Web-based, instructor-facilitated training. This team will develop services and implement new workflow processes using free and open source workflow tools. (U)Tech Stack: Multiple languages Custom tool (U)The focus of this TTO is to provide proactive management of CMS and EIS systems, applications and services. While increasing operational awareness through its application performance monitoring and alerting capabilities, allowing CMS support personnel to perform rapid root cause analysis and proactively address problems before they become critical. Application performance metrics are captured and retained in order to provide an objective performance history which allows developers to (U)25 Monitoring review potential bottlenecks, thus enabling application optimization. Performance alerts can be integrated as monitoring feedback into an application's continuous delivery lifecycle. (U)Tech Stack: AppDynamics, MicroFocus BPM, AWS, MicroFocus OA, (U)Canvas, MicroFocus 0MB (Operations Bridge Manager), Centos, (U)MicroFocus SiteScope, Elasticsearch , Selenium, Groovy, Splunk, (U)Jenkins, Terraform, Kibana, ToolMetrics, Kubernetes, Windows (U)2019, Logstash https://freedomconsultinggroup.sharepoint.com/sites/DragonConfettiStaffing2/SitePages/DRAGONCONFETTI-TTO-Overviews.aspx 12/17 (U)4/2/25, 1:11 PM DRAGONCONFETTI TTO Overviews (U)26 ESuS The focus of this TTO is to manage and operate activities necessary (U)Sustainment to ensure systems under the Enterprise Support Services (ESuS) program continue to be available and to operate effectively. This task also encompasses the activities necessary to upgrade or modify sustainment systems to proactively implement necessary changes to ensure effective and responsive operations. (U)Additionally, this task includes developing and implementing new approaches to improve system sustainment to proactively identify and correct potential issues before outages occur, reduce the time required for outages required to implement upgrades, minimize the number of changes required by sustained systems, collect and report metrics describing system availability and usability, and implement process changes focused on improving system sustainment. (U)Current systems under the ESuS program include: (U)SQUADCHECK (U)Tech Stack: COTS (SQL), SQL Server 12, Microsoft, Silverlight, (U)Microsoft. Net, Framework 4 Client, SharePoint, CASPORT, (U)TLS, Microsoft Office, Adobe (U)Protocol Event Management Systems (POEMS) (U)Tech Stack: Bizflow with Java based customizations, (U)Corporate SharePoint, document storage, Oracle 12C database (U)IP Management Tool - (TTP) (U)Tech Stack: JS Angular, SQL,html, Java, Apache Tomcat (U)Financial Disclosure Reporting System (FDRS) (U)Tech Stack: MEAN stack, Mongo, Express, Angular, Node (U)Talent Dashboard (U)Tech Stack: ExpressJS, NodeJS, redis, bootstrap, JS, D3 JS, (U)NVD3 JS (U)Workflow Orchestration (eIDMS, TrackIT) (U)Tech Stack: Cold Fusion (Custom Developed), SQL Server (U)2016 (U)Event Registration System (ERS) (U)Tech Stack: Oracle, .ASP (U)Combined Federal Campaign (CFC) (U)Tech Stack: Oracle, .ASP (U)IDEAPOP (U)Tech Stack: Languages: PHP 5.3 and Angular 1.2.2, O/S: (U)Linux, Database: MySQL 5.1, Web Tier: Apache (U)Readiness Assessment Tool (U)Tech Stack: PEGA, Oracle, RHEL (U)MEGASTOCK: an application that maintains the agency’s supply inventory and allows for tracking and requesting of https://freedomconsultinggroup.sharepoint.com/sites/DragonConfettiStaffing2/SitePages/DRAGONCONFETTI-TTO-Overviews.aspx 13/17 (U)4/2/25, 1:11 PM DRAGONCONFETTI TTO Overviews parts and equipment that are stored in stockrooms and warehouses throughout the Enterprise. (U)Key Technology: Python; UI Path (U)34 Financial & The focus of this TTO includes the managing and operating (U)Resource activities necessary to ensure systems under the Financial Resource (U)Management Management (FRMS) program continue to be available and to (U)Solutions operate effectively. This task also encompasses the activities necessary to upgrade or modify sustainment systems to proactively implement necessary changes to ensure effective and responsive operations. Additionally, this task includes developing and implementing new approaches to improve system sustainment to proactively identify and correct potential issues before outages occur, reduce the time required for outages required to implement upgrades, minimize the number of changes required by sustained systems, collect and report metrics describing system availability and usability, and implement process changes focused on improving system sustainment. (U)Current systems under the BAMS program that need to be sustained are: (U)PAPERZEPHYR: An application to standardize and improve (U)Government visibility into Contractor expenditures. This application supports the financial and business organizations within the Agency. PAPERZEPHYR (to include AP Accrual, Contract (U)Data Report, and Burn Rate modules) shall be supported using government approved tools. job.clearanceLevel: Workflow and Verification System (CWVS): A web-based system encompassing the clearance and adjudication processes for contractors, NSA/CSS applicants, periodic reinvestigations, and military cases. Tech Stack: JAVA Legacy eQIP: This tool is used to submit contractors, NSA/CSS applicants, and military personnel SF

job.title: Description Connectivity Engineer – Line 3 Function 1 (U) Perform engineering services to enable all aspects of network management from network design through implementation, maintenance, sustainment of existing networks, and identification/correction of complex internetwork issues. Implements and sustains Wide area networks (WANs), Campus Area Networks (CANs), Data Center Networks/Interfaces (DCN/DCI), Network Security (NETSEC) and Tactical/Deployable Networking Systems. Analyzes design, specifications, and related documents. Implements communication system requirements to support the distributed functionality of a networked enterprise environment. Analyzes network characteristics (e.g. traffic connect time, transmission speeds, packet sizes and throughput), troubleshoots problems and recommends modification of network configurations. The Level 3 Connectivity Engineer – Line 3 Function 1 shall possess the following capabilities: (U) Resolve complex network problems, and operate network analyzers, WAN test equipment and network simulators. (U) Perform site surveys, system installations, configuration management, maintenance, modernization, and sustainment of a global Enterprise Network. (U) Perform Connectivity engineering services to pre and post deployment tests. (U) Implement specific network solutions to support server requirements to include load-balancing, VPN’s, firewall contexts, and network address translation (NAT) where appropriate. (U) Minimize network latency and maximize data throughput. (U) Validate new and existing dataflow and data formats. (U) Lead large efforts of dynamic requirements and scale. (U) Work with System engineers,
job.description: Connectivity Engineer – Line 3 Function 1 (U) Perform engineering services to enable all aspects of network management from network design through implementation, maintenance, sustainment of existing networks, and identification/correction of complex internetwork issues. Implements and sustains Wide area networks (WANs), Campus Area Networks (CANs), Data Center Networks/Interfaces (DCN/DCI), Network Security (NETSEC) and Tactical/Deployable Networking Systems. Analyzes design, specifications, and related documents. Implements communication system requirements to support the distributed functionality of a networked enterprise environment. Analyzes network characteristics (e.g. traffic connect time, transmission speeds, packet sizes and throughput), troubleshoots problems and recommends modification of network configurations. (U)The Level 3 Connectivity Engineer – Line 3 Function 1 shall possess the following capabilities: (U) Resolve complex network problems, and operate network analyzers, WAN test equipment and network simulators. (U) Perform site surveys, system installations, configuration management, maintenance, modernization, and sustainment of a global Enterprise Network. (U) Perform Connectivity engineering services to pre and post deployment tests. (U) Implement specific network solutions to support server requirements to include load-balancing, VPN’s, firewall contexts, and network address translation (NAT) where appropriate. (U) Minimize network latency and maximize data throughput. (U) Validate new and existing dataflow and data formats. (U) Lead large efforts of dynamic requirements and scale. (U) Work with System engineers, customers, and cross organizational teams to refine requirements. (U) Coordinate with appropriate dataflow organizations and end-customer recipients, follow-on data repositories and tasking organizations and understand potential adverse impacts on system-wide dataflow. (U) Perform security testing and design evaluations. (U) Employ Government standards to configure the interconnects between data centers, networks and security boundaries. (U) Draft technical requirements, configuration management, and planning documentation. (U) Perform support to QC, QA, and CM services. (U) Design, integrate, configure, deploy, test and maintain numerous types of network devices, interfaces and technologies to include VPN, VRF, VSS, BGP and OSPF routing, and VLAN. (U) Optimize end-to-end system performance (capacity, margins, data quality etc.) and dataflow management. (U) Configure and optimize the network to connect various front-end and back-end components (U) Coordinate with the system engineering and dataflow efforts to ensure proper mission operations with minimal down time. (U) Facilitate the implementation of high-speed, scalable, fault-tolerant network topologies as applicable to WAN and LAN design. (U)Qualifications: Twelve (12) years’ experience in programs and contracts of similar scope, type, and complexity is required. Bachelor’s degree in engineering from an accredited college or university is required. Four (4) years of additional network engineering experience may be substituted for a Bachelor’s degree. Certifications: An IAT level III baseline certification as defined by DoD 8570.01-M is required. An IAT II will be accepted (Security + CE, GSEC, or SSCP) if the candidate obtains an IAT level III (CISA, CISSP, CASP, GCIH, or GCED) within 1 year of contract award and provide expected date for completion. Replacement candidates must meet the IAT level III baseline certification requirement by the date of assignment to contract. A CCNP certification or higher. ITIL ® V3 Foundations.
job.laborCategory: Description Connectivity Engineer – Line 3 Function 1 (U) Perform engineering services to enable all aspects of network management from network design through implementation, maintenance, sustainment of existing networks, and identification/correction of complex internetwork issues. Implements and sustains Wide area networks (WANs), Campus Area Networks (CANs), Data Center Networks/Interfaces (DCN/DCI), Network Security (NETSEC) and Tactical/Deployable Networking Systems. Analyzes design, specifications, and related documents. Implements communication system requirements to support the distributed functionality of a networked enterprise environment. Analyzes network characteristics (e.g. traffic connect time, transmission speeds, packet sizes and throughput), troubleshoots problems and recommends modification of network configurations. The Level 3 Connectivity Engineer – Line 3 Function 1 shall possess the following capabilities: (U) Resolve complex network problems, and operate network analyzers, WAN test equipment and network simulators. (U) Perform site surveys, system installations, configuration management, maintenance, modernization, and sustainment of a global Enterprise Network. (U) Perform Connectivity engineering services to pre and post deployment tests. (U) Implement specific network solutions to support server requirements to include load-balancing, VPN’s, firewall contexts, and network address translation (NAT) where appropriate. (U) Minimize network latency and maximize data throughput. (U) Validate new and existing dataflow and data formats. (U) Lead large efforts of dynamic requirements and scale. (U) Work with System engineers,

job.title: Communications Network Engineer Skill Level 3 (U)
job.description: Monitors, analyzes, troubleshoots, and evaluates hardware, software and other network related problems. Manages the performance and configuration of the global networks and systems. Performs general LAN/CAN/WAN administration. (U) Schedules conversions and cutover. Coordinates with all responsible users and sites to resolve network or system incidents. (U) Responsible for monitoring, tracking and evaluating global Information Technology Infrastructure (ITI) incidents. (U) The Level 3 Communications Network Engineer shall possess the following capabilities: (U) Translates business requirements into telecommunications (e.g., LAN, CAN, WAN, Voice and Video) requirements, designs and orders.(U)(U) Perform in-depth engineering analysis of telecommunications alternatives for Government agencies.(U)(U) Coordinates Telecommunications services to support installations and maintenance. Resolves problems to include performance and configuration. (U) Qualifications (U) Ten (10) years’ experience as an Engineer, applying fundamental skills and concepts using established procedures and standard practices to work problems on contracts of similar scope, type, and complexity is required.(U)(U)(U) A Master’s Degree in a field related to engineering from an accredited college or university is required. In lieu of a Master’s degree, a Bachelor’s degree and an additional four (4) years of experience is required. (U)MASTERS DEGREE: (U)UNIVERSITY: (U)BACHELOR’S DEGREE: (U)UNIVERSITY: (U) Professional Experience (U)Company (U)MM/YYYY–Present (U)Title (U)MM/YYYY–MM/YYYY

job.title: Interconnectivity Engineer Skill Level 3 (U)
job.description: Serves as the Communications/Network Engineering Team Leader, including overall leadership responsibility for Communications/Networks Engineers. Resolves complex network problems, operate network analyzers, WAN test equipment and network simulators. Supports and provides site personnel training on network fundamentals and specific network hardware. Performs network services from network design through implementation, maintenance, and upgrading of existing networks. Analyzes network characteristics (e.g. traffic connect time, transmission speeds, packet sizes and throughput), troubleshoots problems and recommends procurement of and/or modifications to network components. Possess advanced written and verbal communications skills. Serves as the single point of contact for all complex Networking requirements. Assists other project members with analysis and evaluation and with the preparation of recommendations for system improvements, optimization, development, and/or maintenance efforts in the following specialties: information systems architecture; networking; telecommunications; automation; communications protocols. (U) The Interconnectivity Engineer Skill Level 3 shall possess the following capabilities (U) Establishes system information requirements using analysis of the information engineer(s) in the development of enterprise-wide or large-scale information systems. (U) Designs architecture to include the software, hardware, and communications to support the total requirements as well as provide for present and future cross-functional requirements and interfaces. (U) Ensures these systems are compatible and in compliance with the standards for agency IT architectures, and profiles of standards as they apply to the implementation and specific to the solution for the platform, integration with other systems, and the external interfaces. (U) Ensures that the common operating environment is compliant with industry Enterprise standards and best practices. (U) Evaluates analytically and systematically problems of work flows, organization and planning and develops appropriate corrective action. (U) Provides daily supervision and direction to staff. (U) Develops engineering drawings for various reasons including site plans, electrical interconnects and mechanical plans for specialized hardware. (U) Responsible for understanding the needs of the customers and the realities of commercially available IT products, and creating requirements that will allow implementation by the architecture and engineering team and COTS products. (U) Configure and troubleshoot complex converged networks to increase bandwidth, improve response time, maximize performance, and support global operations. (U) Configure complex, end-to-end secure networks, troubleshoot integrated environments, anticipate and (U) respond to network attacks and implement mitigation techniques. (U) Configure and troubleshoot advanced technologies to support service provider networks, such as service provisioning, MPLS, VPNs, service level agreements and advanced security. (U) Configure and troubleshoot storage area networks over variety of interfaces. (U) Configure complex end-to-end telephony network, troubleshooting and resolving VoIP-related problems. (U) Act as the central Point of Contact for the Planning, designing, implementing, operating and troubleshooting of an organization’s wireless network and mobility infrastructure. (U) Qualifications (U) Twelve (12) years of experience in programs and contracts of similar scope, type, and complexity is required. (U) Bachelor’s degree in engineering from an accredited college or university is required. Four (4) years of additional network engineering experience may be substituted for a bachelor's degree. (U) Certifications (U) IAT level II baseline certification as defined by DoD 8570.01-M is required.(U) Current CCIE Certification is required. (U) Domain Area(s) Specialized Requirements: (U) Business: Translating business requirements into network specifications (U) Computer Network Operations: Network Tools; Whats Up Gold and Netflow (U) SIGINT: SIGINT Tools, Common View/Max View, Triton, and TUMMS (U) Professional Experience (U)Company (U)MM/YYYY–Present (U)Title (U)MM/YYYY– MM/YYYY
